title: Navier-Stokes Deep Dive: BKM Criterion, Helicity & Vortex Stretching

key_theorem.statement: For 3D Euler flow, a singularity at T* requires ∫₀^{T*}‖ω‖_∞ dt = ∞. This integral can only diverge if ‖ω‖_∞(t) is EML-∞ near T*: no EML-finite function can satisfy the BKM blowup condition. Helicity H = ∫u·ω dx is EML-2 and conserved (EML-1 bound). The vortex stretching mechanism is EML-2 locally but EML-∞ globally at blowup.

blowup_scenarios.name: Double-exponential blowup
blowup_scenarios.eml_reason: Double-exponential: EML of exp(exp(·)) requires 2 EML levels → EML-∞

blowup_scenarios.name: Non-blowup (smooth solution)
blowup_scenarios.eml_reason: Bounded vorticity: EML-1 (exponential bound suffices); BKM integral finite

bkm_criterion.eml_blowup_barrier: Any EML-finite function has ∫‖ω‖ dt < ∞ on [0,T*) — the BKM divergence is necessarily EML-∞. This is the BKM-EML obstruction theorem.

helicity_conservation.eml_reason: H = ∫u·ω dx: bilinear in EML-2 fields = EML-2

helicity_examples.reason: H = Γ² × (geometric factor) = EML-2 (polynomial × EML-2 integral)
Individual turbulent realizations: EML-∞ paths; ensemble average = 0 = EML-0

woltjer_minimum.theorem: Woltjer (1958): minimum energy MHD state satisfies ∇×B = αB
woltjer_minimum.solution: B is a Beltrami field: eigenfunction of curl
woltjer_minimum.eigenvalue_eq: ∇×B = αB → B = sum of Chandrasekhar-Kendall functions
woltjer_minimum.reason: Beltrami fields are eigenfunctions of curl → involve exp(ik·x) = EML-3

helicity_conservation.helicity_cascade: In turbulence: helicity cascades from large to small scales. Inverse helicity cascade (2.5D): EML-2 inverse power law. Helicity dissipation rate ε_H = ν∫ω·(∇×ω)dx: EML-2.

eml_table.formula: σ = eigenvalue of strain S projected on ω

eml_table.reason: EML-1: single exponential with EML-2 exponent

eml_table.formula: Ω = L² norm squared of vorticity
eml_table.reason: L² functional of EML-2 field = EML-2

eml_table.formula: T* ~ 2/(C√Ω₀): rational function of initial data

eml_table.reason: Blowup is EML-∞ by BKM theorem

vortex_stretching.physical_picture: Vortex tubes stretch and thin under flow → enstrophy concentrates. The local mechanism (EML-2) can cascade to a singularity (EML-∞) if the geometry aligns sufficiently — this is the Navier-Stokes Millennium problem.

eml_depth_summary.EML-0: Linking number (topological invariant); H=0 for unknotted vortex
eml_depth_summary.EML-1: Short-time vorticity growth exp(σt); Woltjer ground state; energy bound
eml_depth_summary.EML-2: Vortex stretching tensor S; enstrophy Ω; helicity H; BKM power-law blowup
eml_depth_summary.EML-3: Beltrami field eigenfunctions of curl; helical Fourier modes
eml_depth_summary.EML-\u221e: Actual blowup singularity; turbulent vorticity realizations

connections.to_session_76: Session 76: Cole-Hopf = EML-2 reduction. Session 85: BKM blowup = EML-∞ obstruction — complementary
connections.to_session_57: Session 57: phase transitions = EML-∞. Session 85: NS blowup = EML-∞ — same universality class
